description: Adaptive personal AI agent — Telegram interface, Obsidian vault, OpenRouter LLM, pluggable transcription (Deepgram default, Voxtral self-hosted future), plus Gmail + Google Calendar BYO OAuth integration with deterministic triage/planning reinforcement workflows through v0.4.1.

epics.title: Project Foundation
epics.description: Initialize the TypeScript project, vault template, setup scripts, and shared types. Everything downstream depends on this foundation being solid.

tickets.title: Initialize TypeScript project with tooling
tickets.description: Set up the public repo structure with TypeScript, strict mode, and essential tooling. This is the first thing built — everything else depends on it.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json with name 'tone', scripts for build, dev, start, and typecheck
tsconfig.json with strict mode, ES2022 target, NodeNext module resolution, outDir 'dist'
.env.example with all required env vars: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, DEEPGRAM_API_KEY, VAULT_PATH, TONE_TIMEZONE, TRANSCRIPTION_PROVIDER (deepgram|voxtral, default deepgram)
.gitignore covers node_modules/, .env, vault/, dist/, *.log
Dev dependencies installed: typescript, tsx, @types/node
Project compiles with zero errors on empty src/index.ts

tickets.title: Create vault template with full directory structure
tickets.description: Build the vault-template directory that ships with the public repo. This is the 'base genome' — the starting point for every Tone instance. Includes seed skill definitions (markdown), config files, and feedback directory structure.
acceptanceCriteria: vault-template/ contains all directories: _inbox, threads, tasks, projects, people, daily, skills, config, feedback
feedback/ has subdirectories: interactions, daily, weekly
feedback/ has seed files: corrections.md, autonomic.md, patterns.md, evolution.md — each with header and empty structure
tasks/ has seed files: active.md, completed.md, someday.md
skills/ has 6 seed skill definitions: capture.md, task.md, draft.md, briefing.md, reflect.md (IMMUTABLE), adapt.md (IMMUTABLE)
Each skill markdown defines: trigger patterns, input/output format, examples, and constraints
config/ has 4 files: about-user.md (template), personality.md (neutral start), reward-signals.md, boundaries.md
boundaries.md clearly lists immutable vs evolvable vs autonomic categories

tickets.title: Build setup and vault init scripts
tickets.description: Create shell scripts for first-run setup, vault initialization, and systemd service management. These scripts make Tone easy to deploy on a fresh Raspberry Pi.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/setup.sh installs Node.js deps, checks for required env vars, runs init-vault if vault doesn't exist
scripts/init-vault.sh copies vault-template to VAULT_PATH, initializes private git repo, creates initial commit tagged 'base-v0.1.0'
scripts/service.sh can install/start/stop/restart Tone as a systemd service
All scripts are idempotent — safe to run multiple times
Scripts check for prerequisites and give clear error messages
init-vault.sh refuses to overwrite an existing vault (safety check)

tickets.title: Define shared types and config module
tickets.description: Create the type definitions and config module that all other source files depend on. Types should precisely model the domain: interactions, skills, feedback events, vault structure. Config module loads and validates env vars.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types.ts exports interfaces: Interaction, SkillDefinition, FeedbackEvent, VaultConfig, LLMTier, RouterResult
Interaction type includes: id, timestamp, userId, input (text/voice), intent, skillUsed, response, feedbackSignal
SkillDefinition models the markdown skill format: name, triggers, inputSchema, outputSchema, constraints, immutable flag
FeedbackEvent covers: corrections, thumbs up/down, implicit signals (engagement timing, draft acceptance)
src/config.ts loads .env, validates all required vars exist, exports typed config object
Config module throws clear errors for missing or invalid env vars
typecheck passes with no errors

epics.title: Core Services
epics.description: Build the four foundational services: LLM client with tiered routing, voice transcription, vault CRUD operations, and Telegram bot setup. These are independent services that the interaction pipeline composes.

tickets.title: LLM client with tiered routing via OpenRouter
tickets.description: Build the OpenRouter LLM client with three-tier model routing. Tier 1 (Gemini Flash) for cheap tasks like intent routing and filing. Tier 2 (Claude Sonnet) for user-facing interactions. Tier 3 (Claude Opus) for self-improvement and code generation.
acceptanceCriteria: src/llm.ts exports functions: complete(prompt, tier), chat(messages, tier), and a streaming variant
Three tiers configured: tier1 (gemini-2.0-flash), tier2 (claude-sonnet-4-5), tier3 (claude-opus-4)
Each request includes proper OpenRouter headers (HTTP-Referer, X-Title)
Handles rate limiting with exponential backoff
Handles API errors gracefully with typed error responses
Token usage tracked and returned with each response
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Pluggable voice transcription service (Deepgram + Voxtral)
tickets.description: Build a pluggable voice transcription service behind a TranscriptionProvider interface. Ship two implementations: Deepgram (cloud API, default for Pi 4) and Voxtral Mini (self-hosted via Mistral, for Beelink/GPU hardware). Provider selected via TRANSCRIPTION_PROVIDER env var. Handles Telegram's OGG/Opus audio format.
acceptanceCriteria: src/transcriber.ts exports TranscriptionProvider interface with transcribe(audioBuffer, mimeType) => TranscriptionResult
TranscriptionResult includes: text, confidence, provider, durationMs
src/transcriber.ts exports createTranscriber(provider) factory that returns the configured provider
Deepgram provider: calls Deepgram API, handles OGG/Opus, returns confidence score
Voxtral provider: calls local/remote Voxtral Mini endpoint (OpenAI-compatible API), handles OGG/Opus
TRANSCRIPTION_PROVIDER env var selects provider ('deepgram' default, 'voxtral' for self-hosted)
VOXTRAL_ENDPOINT env var configures Voxtral URL (e.g. http://localhost:8000/v1) when provider is voxtral
Handles API errors gracefully for both providers
Handles empty/silent audio gracefully (returns empty string, not error)
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Obsidian vault CRUD service
tickets.description: Build the vault service for reading, writing, and querying markdown files in the Obsidian vault. Supports wiki-link resolution, frontmatter parsing, and directory listing. This is how Tone reads and writes its own knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: src/vault.ts exports: readNote, writeNote, appendNote, listNotes, searchNotes, resolveWikiLink
readNote parses frontmatter (YAML) and returns structured { frontmatter, content } object
writeNote creates/overwrites markdown files with optional frontmatter
appendNote adds content to existing file (for logs, daily notes)
listNotes returns files in a directory with metadata (modified date, size)
searchNotes does full-text search across vault files
resolveWikiLink converts [[note-name]] to file path, handling ambiguity
All paths validated to stay within vault root (no path traversal)
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Telegram bot setup and utilities
tickets.description: Set up the Telegram bot with Telegraf, handle text and voice messages, wire reaction callbacks (thumbs up/down as feedback signals), and build formatting utilities for structured responses.
acceptanceCriteria: src/index.ts creates Telegraf bot, registers handlers for text, voice, and callback queries
src/utils/telegram.ts exports: formatBriefing, formatTaskList, sendWithReactions, downloadVoice
downloadVoice fetches voice message file from Telegram API and returns Buffer
sendWithReactions attaches inline keyboard with thumbs up/down buttons
Reaction callbacks emit typed FeedbackEvent objects
Bot handles errors gracefully (logs error, sends user-friendly message)
Bot responds to /start with a welcome message
typecheck passes

epics.title: Interaction Pipeline
epics.description: Build the full message-handling pipeline: intent routing, skill loading and execution, and feedback logging. TONE-012 is the integration ticket that wires everything end-to-end.

tickets.title: Intent router with confidence scoring
tickets.description: Build the intent router that classifies incoming messages into skill intents (capture, task, draft, chat) with confidence scores. Uses Tier 1 LLM for fast, cheap classification. Falls back to chat skill when confidence is below threshold.
acceptanceCriteria: src/router.ts exports route(message) returning RouterResult with { intent, confidence, extractedEntities }
Uses Tier 1 LLM (Gemini Flash) for classification — fast and cheap
Recognizes intents: capture, task, draft, chat, rollback, introspection
Confidence threshold configurable (default 0.7) — below threshold falls back to chat
Extracts entities from message (task description, draft recipient, capture topic)
Prompt includes loaded skill definitions for context (skill triggers from vault)
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Skill loader and capture skill implementation
tickets.description: Build the skill loader that reads skill definitions from vault markdown files and provides them to the router and skill executor. Implement the capture skill as the first concrete skill — it files thoughts, voice notes, and quick ideas into the appropriate vault location.
acceptanceCriteria: src/skills/types.ts defines SkillHandler interface: { name, execute(input, context) => SkillResult }
src/skills/loader.ts reads skill markdown from vault/skills/, parses into SkillDefinition objects
Loader caches parsed skills, invalidates cache when vault files change
Loader returns skill metadata (triggers, constraints) for the router to use
src/skills/capture.ts implements capture skill: classifies thought topic, selects target file, appends to vault
Capture skill uses Tier 1 LLM to determine filing location (which thread or _inbox)
Capture skill supports both text and transcribed voice input
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Task, draft, and chat skill implementations
tickets.description: Implement the three remaining seed skills. Task skill manages a simple task system in the vault. Draft skill generates message drafts for contacts. Chat skill is the general-purpose conversational fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: src/skills/task.ts handles: add task, complete task, list tasks, move to someday
Task skill reads/writes tasks/active.md, tasks/completed.md, tasks/someday.md
src/skills/draft.ts generates message drafts using Tier 2 LLM with context from people/ notes
Draft skill looks up recipient in people/ directory for context and communication style
src/skills/chat.ts handles general conversation using Tier 2 LLM with vault context
Chat skill loads personality.md and recent conversation context for consistent responses
All skills return structured SkillResult matching the types.ts interface
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Feedback logger and wire full interaction pipeline
tickets.description: Build the feedback logger that records every interaction as JSONL, tracks outcome signals, and logs corrections. Then wire the complete pipeline in index.ts: message → transcribe (if voice) → route → execute skill → respond → log interaction → attach feedback buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: src/feedback.ts exports: logInteraction, logCorrection, logFeedbackSignal
logInteraction appends JSONL to vault/feedback/interactions/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl
Each interaction log entry matches the Interaction type from types.ts
logCorrection appends to vault/feedback/corrections.md with timestamp and context
logFeedbackSignal records thumbs up/down against the interaction ID
src/index.ts wires the full pipeline: voice → transcribe → route → skill → respond → log
Telegram reaction callbacks (thumbs up/down) call logFeedbackSignal
Error handling at each pipeline stage — failures don't crash the bot
typecheck passes

epics.title: Proactive Loops
epics.description: Build the three feedback loops that make Tone self-improving: morning briefing, nightly circadian review (Loop 2), and weekly adaptation (Loop 3). Also includes the evolution module for vault git operations, which all loops depend on.

tickets.title: Morning briefing loop
tickets.description: Build the morning briefing that runs on a cron schedule. Aggregates active tasks, recent thread activity, and upcoming events into a formatted Telegram message. Briefing format and timing are autonomic — Tone can adjust them based on engagement patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: src/loops/briefing.ts exports a generateBriefing() function and a scheduleBriefing() cron setup
Briefing reads: tasks/active.md, recent daily/ notes, thread activity, config/personality.md
Uses Tier 2 LLM to synthesize briefing content in the personality's voice
Sends formatted briefing via Telegram using formatBriefing utility
Briefing includes sections: priorities, active threads, pending tasks
Cron schedule configurable via env var (default 07:30 local time)
Briefing engagement (read timing, response) tracked as implicit feedback
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Nightly circadian review (Loop 2)
tickets.description: Build the nightly review that runs at 23:00. Evaluates the day's interactions, tracks outcomes, updates patterns.md, makes autonomic adjustments (briefing timing, verbosity), and commits everything to the vault git repo.
acceptanceCriteria: src/loops/nightly.ts exports generateNightlyReview() and scheduleNightly() cron setup
Reads all interaction JSONL logs for the current day
Aggregates: total interactions, positive/negative signals, skill usage distribution
Uses Tier 3 LLM to analyse patterns and generate daily reflection
Writes daily reflection to vault/feedback/daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md
Updates vault/feedback/patterns.md with newly observed patterns
Makes autonomic adjustments (briefing timing, verbosity) based on engagement data
Records autonomic adjustments in vault/feedback/autonomic.md
Commits all changes to vault git repo with 'nightly:' prefix commit message
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Evolution module (vault git operations)
tickets.description: Build the evolution module that wraps git operations on the vault repo. This is the foundation for all feedback loops — every mutation Tone makes to itself becomes a git commit. Uses simple-git for programmatic git access.
acceptanceCriteria: src/evolution.ts exports: commit, tag, createBranch, mergeBranch, diff, log, rollback, getCurrentTag
commit(message, type) creates a typed commit: 'correction', 'nightly', 'adapt'
tag(name) creates a named tag (e.g., 'week-08', 'snapshot/good-at-briefings')
createBranch(name) creates a branch for weekly adaptation proposals
mergeBranch(name) merges an adaptation branch back to main
diff(fromRef, toRef, paths?) returns structured diff information
rollback(ref, paths?) restores files from a previous state as a new commit
All operations work on the vault repo path (not the code repo)
simple-git added as dependency
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Weekly adaptation loop (Loop 3) with approval flow
tickets.description: Build the weekly Friday review that analyses the entire week's data, proposes behavioural changes on a git branch, sends a summary via Telegram, and waits for user approval. Approved changes merge to main. Rejected changes are logged with reasoning.
acceptanceCriteria: src/loops/weekly.ts exports generateWeeklyReview() and scheduleWeekly() cron setup
Reads all daily reviews and interaction logs for the past 7 days
Uses Tier 3 LLM to identify trends, propose skill modifications, suggest personality tweaks
Creates a git branch (e.g., 'adapt/week-08') with proposed changes
Tags current main as 'week-NN' before branching (snapshot for rollback)
Writes detailed proposal to vault/feedback/weekly/YYYY-WNN.md
Sends formatted summary to Telegram with approve/reject inline buttons
On approve: merges branch to main, commits with 'adapt:' prefix
On reject: logs rejection reasoning, deletes branch, keeps data for next review
Cron runs Friday at 15:00 local time (configurable)
typecheck passes

epics.title: Rollback & Introspection
epics.description: Complete the feedback loop system with Loop 1 (immediate corrections committed to git), rollback commands via Telegram, and the 'what's changed' introspection feature. These give the user full visibility and control over Tone's evolution.

tickets.title: Loop 1 — immediate corrections with git commits
tickets.description: Enhance the feedback logger to detect corrections (user fixing Tone's behaviour) and automatically commit them to the vault git repo. Each correction becomes a 'heartbeat commit' — small, frequent, immediately applied.
acceptanceCriteria: src/feedback.ts enhanced: logCorrection now also commits to vault git via evolution module
Correction detection: identifies when user is correcting a previous response (explicit 'no, do X' or re-filing)
Each correction committed with 'correction:' prefix and descriptive message
Corrections update vault/feedback/corrections.md with the correction and learned heuristic
Correction context stored: what Tone did wrong, what the user wanted, the rule to learn
Corrections are available for nightly review (Loop 2) aggregation
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Rollback via Telegram and named snapshots
tickets.description: Implement rollback commands accessible via natural language in Telegram. User can roll back to a named state, revert a specific skill, or create a named snapshot. All rollbacks are new commits (history is never rewritten).
acceptanceCriteria: Router recognizes rollback intents: 'go back to week N', 'revert briefing', 'save this state as X'
src/evolution.ts rollback functions invoked from router for rollback intents
Full rollback: restores all skills/config from a tag or commit ref
Surgical rollback: restores specific files (e.g., just skills/briefing.md)
Before rollback: shows user what would change (diff in plain English via LLM)
User must confirm rollback via Telegram button before it's applied
Named snapshots: 'save state as X' creates tag 'snapshot/X'
Pre-rollback state tagged automatically (e.g., 'pre-rollback-TIMESTAMP') for safety
typecheck passes

tickets.title: 'What's changed' introspection and evolution log
tickets.description: Implement the introspection feature: user asks 'what's changed about you since week N?' and gets a plain-English summary of Tone's evolution. Also build the evolution log writer that maintains the narrative account in feedback/evolution.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Router recognizes introspection intents: 'what's changed since X', 'how have you evolved'
src/evolution.ts diff output piped through Tier 2 LLM to generate plain-English summary
Summary describes: what skills changed, personality shifts, autonomic adjustments, approval history
Evolution log writer appends weekly summaries to vault/feedback/evolution.md
Evolution log format matches the doc spec: state at start, changes, state at end, reward trend
Weekly loop (TONE-016) calls evolution log writer after each review cycle
typecheck passes

epics.title: Gmail BYO OAuth Integration
epics.description: Integrate Gmail using a BYO OAuth model only. Each user configures their own Google OAuth app credentials locally, then Tone can summarize inbox, draft replies, and send only with explicit confirmation.

tickets.title: BYO Gmail OAuth onboarding and config surface
tickets.description: Add Gmail configuration and onboarding for per-user OAuth credentials. No shared platform OAuth client is used. Tone must continue working when Gmail is not configured.
acceptanceCriteria: .env.example includes Gmail vars: GMAIL_ENABLED, GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET, GMAIL_REDIRECT_URI, GMAIL_TOKEN_PATH
src/config.ts validates Gmail vars only when GMAIL_ENABLED=true
src/onboard.ts prompts for optional Gmail setup and stores values in local env file
README documents BYO OAuth setup and explicitly states each user creates their own Google OAuth app
App starts normally with Gmail disabled
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Gmail auth and token lifecycle service
tickets.description: Build Gmail OAuth token handling service: generate auth URL, exchange authorization code, refresh tokens, and expose connection status with typed errors.
acceptanceCriteria: src/integrations/gmail/auth.ts exports methods for startAuth, exchangeCode, refreshAccessToken, getConnectionStatus
Access token refresh occurs automatically before API calls when expired
Token file is stored outside git-tracked project files (default under Tone home path)
Token storage permissions are restrictive (user-readable only)
Typed errors include invalid_grant, missing_credentials, refresh_failed, and disconnected states
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Gmail API client for inbox, threads, drafts, and send
tickets.description: Implement a Gmail client wrapper with typed request/response models for inbox retrieval, thread detail retrieval, draft creation, and reply sending.
acceptanceCriteria: src/integrations/gmail/client.ts exports listInbox, getThread, createReplyDraft, sendReply
Supports pagination when listing inbox messages
Maps Google API errors to typed application errors for user-safe messaging
Implements retry/backoff for transient 429/5xx responses
Respects least-privilege scopes required for read/draft/send flow
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Email skill and router wiring for triage + draft
tickets.description: Add an email skill and route email intents to it so users can request inbox triage and draft replies from Telegram.
acceptanceCriteria: Router recognizes email-related requests and maps them to email handling intent/entities
src/skills/email.ts supports inbox summary and draft generation for selected message/thread
Email skill uses Gmail client and degrades gracefully when Gmail is disconnected
Skill output includes stable reference needed for later send confirmation
Skill is registered in src/skills/index.ts and invoked by the main pipeline
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Email send confirmation callbacks in Telegram
tickets.description: Implement explicit confirm/cancel callbacks for email sending. Sending a draft must always require user confirmation via Telegram inline controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Email send requires explicit callback confirmation before any Gmail send API call
Cancel path leaves the draft persisted and records cancellation reason/context
Duplicate callback events are idempotent and do not send multiple emails
Success/failure/cancel outcomes are logged through feedback pipeline
User sees clear Telegram confirmation status messages
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Vault email sync and briefing integration
tickets.description: Persist minimal email context into the private vault and include actionable email priorities in the morning briefing.
acceptanceCriteria: src/integrations/gmail/sync.ts writes minimal thread snapshots to vault/email/threads
Generated drafts and decisions are written to vault/email/drafts
Briefing includes email section with top needs_reply and waiting_on_them items
Path validation prevents traversal and keeps writes inside vault root
If Gmail is disconnected, briefing still runs and reports email as unavailable
typecheck passes

epics.title: Email Triage and Reinforcement
epics.description: Implement a simple deterministic triage and reinforcement loop for email so Tone can prioritize what needs a reply, remind at useful times, and improve from feedback without overcomplicated ML.

tickets.title: Deterministic triage labels and priority scoring
tickets.description: Create a deterministic triage model with fixed labels and transparent scoring for actionable email prioritization.
acceptanceCriteria: Triage labels implemented: needs_reply, waiting_on_them, fyi, no_reply_needed
Priority score for needs_reply uses deterministic features: sender importance, direct questions, deadline language, thread age, unread count
Each scored item includes human-readable reasoning for why it was prioritized
Triage output is consumable by email skill and briefing loop
Thresholds/weights are configurable (single config file)
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Reminder cadence and quick actions
tickets.description: Implement simple reminder cadence and action shortcuts: morning top list, midday unresolved nudge, evening carry-over recap, with snooze/remind controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Morning briefing includes top 5 needs_reply items
Midday reminder surfaces unresolved high-priority items
Evening recap lists carry-over items for next day
Telegram quick actions supported: draft, send(confirm), snooze 4h, remind tomorrow, mark no-reply, done
Actions map back to triage state consistently
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Reinforcement instrumentation for triage outcomes
tickets.description: Capture explicit and implicit signals from triage/reply workflows and feed them into nightly analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback events added for triage outcomes (accepted, edited, sent after reminder, repeated snooze, ignored urgent)
Outcome events are logged in feedback/interactions JSONL with interaction linkage
Nightly review aggregates email-specific outcome metrics
No existing feedback event handling regressions
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Weekly adaptation tuning for triage weights and thresholds
tickets.description: Use weekly loop outcomes to propose small, bounded updates to triage weights/thresholds, rather than broad behavioral rewrites.
acceptanceCriteria: Weekly review includes triage scorecard section based on email outcomes
Proposals modify only triage config weights/thresholds within predefined ranges
Approval flow remains required before applying any triage config mutation
Rejected proposals remain logged for later analysis
typecheck passes

tickets.title: v0.4.0 hardening, runbook, and release checklist
tickets.description: Finalize reliability hardening, documentation, and release gates for v0.4.0 Gmail integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Runbook documents BYO OAuth setup, common failures, and recovery steps
Reliability checks cover quota/rate limits, token expiration, and transient API errors
Smoke test checklist covers connect, triage, draft, send-confirm, reminder loop, and rollback
Release metadata aligned: package version, changelog, and git tag/release notes
typecheck passes

epics.title: Google Calendar Context and Planning
epics.description: Integrate Google Calendar alongside Gmail so Tone can understand upcoming commitments, support meeting prep, and synthesize day/week planning with tasks and inbox urgency.

tickets.title: Calendar BYO OAuth onboarding and config surface
tickets.description: Extend onboarding and config for optional Google Calendar access under the same BYO OAuth model. Calendar remains optional and Tone must run normally when it is disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: .env.example includes Calendar vars: CALENDAR_ENABLED, CALENDAR_SCOPES, CALENDAR_SYNC_WINDOW_DAYS
src/config.ts validates Calendar vars only when CALENDAR_ENABLED=true
src/onboard.ts prompts for optional Calendar setup and preserves BYO OAuth guidance
README documents required Calendar permissions and local token storage constraints
App starts normally with Calendar disabled
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Google Calendar API client for events and free-busy windows
tickets.description: Implement a Calendar client wrapper with typed models for upcoming events, event details, and free/busy availability context.
acceptanceCriteria: src/integrations/calendar/client.ts exports listUpcomingEvents, getEventDetails, getFreeBusy
Supports date-window queries (today, next 7 days) with timezone normalization
Maps Google API errors to typed application errors for user-safe messaging
Implements retry/backoff for transient 429/5xx responses
Uses least-privilege read-only Calendar scopes for this phase
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Calendar skill and router wiring for day/week planning
tickets.description: Add a calendar skill and route planning intents so users can ask for today's plan, weekly plan, and next-meeting prep from Telegram.
acceptanceCriteria: Router recognizes planning intents and maps them to calendar handling intent/entities
src/skills/calendar.ts supports today agenda, week preview, and next-meeting prep
Planning output combines event timeline with active tasks and top needs_reply email items
Calendar skill degrades gracefully when Calendar is disconnected
Skill is registered in src/skills/index.ts and invoked by the main pipeline
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Briefing and reminder synthesis across email + calendar + tasks
tickets.description: Upgrade briefing/reminder outputs so they jointly reason over meetings, inbox urgency, and task carry-over.
acceptanceCriteria: Morning briefing includes key meetings, prep-needed items, and top needs_reply email threads
Midday reminder includes unresolved high-priority emails and next-meeting prep prompts
Evening recap highlights carry-over tasks/emails and tomorrow's first commitments
If Calendar is disconnected, briefing still runs and reports calendar as unavailable
typecheck passes

tickets.title: Planning feedback instrumentation and weekly tuning hooks
tickets.description: Capture planning outcome signals and feed them into nightly/weekly loops so scheduling suggestions improve with approval-gated adaptation.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback events added for planning outcomes (plan_followed, prep_completed, prep_missed, calendar_conflict_detected)
Outcome events are logged in feedback/interactions JSONL with interaction linkage
Nightly review aggregates calendar-planning outcome metrics
Weekly review can propose bounded planning heuristic updates with approval flow preserved
No existing feedback event handling regressions
typecheck passes
